step_by_step_thinking: Randomization in a clinical trial helps reduce biases like confounding variables and ensures that participants are comparable between different arms of the study. It does not inherently increase the external validity of the trial, although proper design and conduct might enhance it. Therefore:
raw_response:
Randomization in a clinical trial helps reduce biases like confounding variables and ensures that participants are comparable between different arms of the study. It does not inherently increase the external validity of the trial, although proper design and conduct might enhance it. Therefore:
\boxed{D} The statement "Increases external validity of the trial" is not necessarily true regarding randomization alone. While randomization contributes to valid internal comparisons, enhancing external validity requires additional factors such as representative sample size and adequate follow-up. Randomization focuses mainly on minimizing selection and confounding biases rather than directly increasing external applicability. Thus, D is incorrect according to standard definitions and practices in clinical trials.
